outbound_official.note: No official Cyprus government outbound visa-list page located. The Cyprus Ministry of Foreign Affairs (gov.cy/mfa) publishes only inbound visa policy pages (who may enter Cyprus without a prior consular visa). No dedicated page listing countries where Cyprus ordinary-passport holders may travel visa-free, visa-on-arrival, e-visa, or ETA was found on any official Cyprus government domain (gov.cy, mfa.gov.cy, or any embassy/high-commission subdomain). Cyprus is an EU member state; outbound travel freedom derives from EU freedom-of-movement law and bilateral agreements negotiated at EU level rather than from a Cyprus-government-published outbound list. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_types.name: Cyprus National Short-Stay Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Permits entry to Cyprus for tourism, leisure, visiting family/friends, or short business visits. Valid only for Cyprus - not for the Schengen Area, as Cyprus is an EU member but not part of the Schengen zone.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of countries listed on the Cyprus MFA visa-required list. Cyprus issues its own national short-stay visa; it does not participate in the Schengen visa system. Standard fee is EUR 30 for single-entry and EUR 60 for multiple-entry (approximate; exact fee schedule set by Civil Registry and Migration Department). Applications lodged at Cyprus embassies, consulates, or honorary consulates. No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists. Holders of a valid double or multiple-entry Schengen visa, or residence permit/visa issued by Bulgaria, Romania, or Croatia, may enter Cyprus without a separate Cyprus visa (Turkey and Azerbaijan nationals excepted).

visa_types.name: Cyprus Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through the international transit zone of a Cyprus airport without entering Cyprus territory. Required for nationals of specific countries whose international travel routes pass through Larnaka or Pafos airports.
visa_types.notes: Airport transit visa (ATV) is required for nationals of countries specified by the Cyprus Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Exemptions apply for holders of valid Schengen/EU/Bulgarian/Romanian/Croatian visas or residence permits, diplomatic passport holders per bilateral agreements, and flight crew with valid crew member licences. Applications made at Cyprus embassies or consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Business Short-Stay Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Permits entry to Cyprus for short-term business purposes including meetings, negotiations, trade fairs, conferences, and similar commercial activities. Issued under the same Type C national visa framework as the tourist visa.
visa_types.notes: Business visitors from visa-required countries must obtain a national short-stay visa from a Cyprus consulate. The same Type C visa category covers both tourism and short-term business purposes. For business activities requiring employment or professional engagement in Cyprus, a work permit (Type D long-stay visa plus work permit) is needed instead.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Long-Stay Work Visa and Temporary Residence/Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to reside and work in Cyprus under an employer-sponsored arrangement. Covers standard employment in all sectors including domestic workers, technical workers, and seasonal agricultural workers.
visa_types.notes: Third-country nationals must secure a job offer from a Cyprus employer. The employer applies for a work permit to the Civil Registry and Migration Department (CRMD). A prior labour market test is generally required (newspaper/JobsBoard advertisement for 30 days) unless the applicant qualifies under the BFU Fast-Track Highly Qualified Employees Scheme. Upon approval, the applicant receives a Type D entry visa and then a temporary residence and work permit (annual renewal). Permit fee is approximately EUR 70 per year for the Alien Registration Certificate (ARC).

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card Cyprus
visa_types.purpose: Permits highly skilled third-country nationals to work in Cyprus in eligible high-skill sectors (ICT, pharmaceuticals for research, and shipping excluding captains and crew), with EU-wide intra-EU mobility rights.
visa_types.notes: Launched July 7, 2025. Minimum gross annual salary EUR 43,632. Eligible sectors per Ministerial Decree: ICT, pharmaceuticals (research only), and shipping (excluding ship captains and crew). No quota. Requires third-level education qualification or equivalent professional experience plus a valid work contract. Provides EU-wide intra-EU mobility rights (excluding Denmark and Ireland). Family reunification permits equal in duration to Blue Card. Application form MBCEU1 available on the Migration Department website.

visa_types.name: Business Facilitation Unit (BFU) Fast-Track Highly Qualified Employees Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Permits highly skilled third-country nationals to work in Cyprus companies registered with the Business Facilitation Unit (Companies of Foreign Interests) without a labour market test, with reduced processing times.
visa_types.notes: Valid until December 31, 2026. Companies registered with the BFU (Ministry of Energy, Commerce and Industry) can employ any number of highly skilled third-country nationals without undergoing a labour market test. Applications processed by the Civil Registry and Migration Department within one month. Companies must be registered with the BFU and meet eligibility criteria (Companies of Foreign Interests or related qualifying entities).

visa_types.name: Cyprus Student Visa and Temporary Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to enter and reside in Cyprus for the purpose of pursuing studies at a recognized Cyprus educational institution (university, college, or vocational training centre).
visa_types.notes: Students must produce an unconditional letter of acceptance from a recognized Cyprus educational institution, proof of sufficient financial resources, and health insurance. The student permit is issued annually and renewed for the duration of studies. Students may work up to 20 hours per week during term time. Issued by the Civil Registry and Migration Department. Permit fee approximately EUR 70/year for ARC.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Digital Nomad Visa Scheme
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals who work remotely using telecommunications technology for employers or clients located entirely outside Cyprus to reside in Cyprus for up to 3 years.
visa_types.notes: Open to non-EU/EEA nationals only. Minimum monthly net income EUR 3,500 (after taxes and contributions); increased by 20% for a spouse/partner and 15% per dependent child. Residence initially granted for 1 year, renewable for up to 2 further years (3 years total). Maximum 500 permits total across all holders. Family members may reside but cannot work or conduct economic activity in Cyprus. Applicant must enter Cyprus on a valid entry visa (national Type C), then apply for the Digital Nomad temporary residence permit within 3 months of arrival at the Migration Department in Nicosia.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Investor Immigration Permit (Regulation 6(2) – Permanent Residence by Investment)
visa_types.purpose: Grants permanent residence in Cyprus to third-country nationals who make a qualifying investment of at least EUR 300,000 in Cyprus real estate, business share capital, or collective investment funds.
visa_types.notes: 4th Revision criteria in force from May 2, 2023. Four qualifying investment categories each requiring EUR 300,000 (plus VAT where applicable): (A) new residential property (first sale from developer), (B) non-residential or resale real estate, (C) share capital of a Cyprus-registered company employing at least 5 people, (D) units in a licensed Cyprus collective investment scheme (AIF/AIFLNP/RAIF). Applicant must demonstrate a secured annual income of at least EUR 50,000 from abroad (plus EUR 15,000 per dependent spouse, EUR 10,000 per minor child). Application fee EUR 500 plus EUR 70 per person for ARC. The permit is of unlimited validity; the residence card is issued for 10 years and renewed on expiry. Annual monitoring of investment maintenance required; criminal record certificate every 3 years. Permit lapses if holder fails to enter Cyprus within 1 year of approval or is absent for 2 years.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Immigration Permit for Category F Retirees
visa_types.purpose: Grants immigration permits to retired non-EU/EEA nationals who wish to reside permanently in Cyprus without engaging in any employment or business activity, based on demonstrated passive income from abroad.
visa_types.notes: Known informally as the 'Category F' immigration permit under the Aliens and Immigration Law. Applicant must prove secure annual income from abroad sufficient to support themselves and dependents in Cyprus (income threshold set by authorities; historically approximately EUR 9,568/year for single person plus EUR 4,613 for each additional family member). Applicant must not engage in any employment in Cyprus. The immigration permit has unlimited duration. Applications submitted to the Civil Registry and Migration Department.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Family Reunification Permit
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals who are family members of a legally resident third-country national in Cyprus to join and reside with the sponsor in Cyprus.
visa_types.notes: Governed by Council Directive 2003/86/EC on the right to family reunification, transposed into Cyprus law. Eligible family members include spouse and minor children under 18. Sponsor must hold a valid temporary residence permit for at least 1 year and have a reasonable prospect of obtaining permanent residence. Sponsor must demonstrate adequate accommodation, health insurance, and stable financial resources. Family members of EU Blue Card holders benefit from more favourable conditions.

visa_types.name: Visa Waiver for Diplomatic and Official Passport Holders
visa_types.purpose: Exempts holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports of designated countries from the visa requirement for entry to Cyprus, in accordance with bilateral agreements or Cyprus MFA policy.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic passport holders of all countries with which Cyprus maintains diplomatic relations are generally exempt from visa requirements. Bilateral agreements cover diplomatic and official passports of over 30 countries including Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, China, Cuba, Egypt, Georgia, India, Iran, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kosovo, Kuwait, Lebanon, Marshall Islands, Mexico, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, North Macedonia, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Serbia, Seychelles, Syria, Ukraine, Vanuatu, Vietnam, and others. Holders of UN laissez-passer and Council of Europe laissez-passer are also exempt. Subject to Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations.

visa_types.name: Cyprus Fast-Track Naturalisation for Highly Skilled Employees
visa_types.purpose: Provides an accelerated naturalisation pathway for highly skilled foreign employees of Companies of Foreign Interests registered with the BFU, with reduced residency requirements and expedited processing.
visa_types.notes: Amendments to the Civil Registry Law gazetted December 19, 2023, reduced the standard 7-year residency requirement to 3-4 years for highly skilled workers of BFU-registered companies. Fast-track naturalisation processing maximum of 8 months with a EUR 5,000 application fee. This is a naturalisation pathway (citizenship), not a visa or residence permit in itself, but is the end-stage of the immigration pathway for this category. Access via the Civil Registry and Migration Department.

visa_free.nationality: All EU Member States (Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden)
visa_free.notes: EU citizens may enter with national ID card bearing a photo; no visa or passport required. Freedom of movement applies - no fixed cap.

visa_policy.notes: Cyprus is NOT part of the Schengen Area; its visas grant entry only to Cyprus, not to other EU/Schengen states. A Cyprus short-stay visa covers stays up to 90 days. The complete list of visa-required nationalities is maintained on the official MFA page. Turkey nationals require a visa and are excluded from the Schengen/Bulgarian/Croatian/Romanian permit exemption. Azerbaijan nationals are also excluded from the credential exemption. No e-visa or ETA system is operated by Cyprus. Kosovo was added to the visa-free list (biometric passport holders) per a June 2024 MFA document. Taiwan is listed under entities not recognised as states by at least one EU member state but is on the no-visa list. The official no-visa page was last updated 09 June 2024.

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid double or multiple-entry Schengen visa may enter Cyprus for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. This exemption does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan.

credential.subtype: residence permit issued by Schengen Member State

conditional_access.conditions: Residence permit must be valid; stay may not exceed 90 days in any 180-day period; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 15 May 2014.

credential.subtype: double or multiple-entry Bulgarian national visa

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid double or multiple-entry Bulgarian national visa may enter Cyprus for up to 90 days. Does NOT apply to Turkey and Azerbaijan nationals.
Residence permit must be valid; stay may not exceed 90 days; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU.

credential.subtype: double or multiple-entry Croatian national visa

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid (double or multiple-entry); stay may not exceed 90 days; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU.
Residence permit must be valid; stay may not exceed 90 days; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU.

credential.subtype: double or multiple-entry Romanian national visa

conditional_access.conditions: Visa must be valid (double or multiple-entry); stay may not exceed 90 days; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU.
Residence permit must be valid; stay may not exceed 90 days; does NOT apply to nationals of Turkey and Azerbaijan. Legal basis: Decision No. 565/2014/EU.

credential.subtype: travel document issued to recognised refugee under 1951 Convention by a Schengen member state or Annex II/539/2001 state

conditional_access.conditions: Travel document must be issued by a Schengen member state OR by a state listed in Annex II to Council Regulation 539/2001 (as amended) to a recognised refugee under the Convention relating to the Status of Refugees of 28 July 1951.

credential.subtype: travel document issued to stateless person under 1954 Convention by a Schengen member state (holder must reside in that state)

conditional_access.conditions: Travel document must be issued by a Schengen member state to a stateless person under the Convention relating to the Status of Stateless Persons of 28 September 1954, and the holder must be residing in that issuing Schengen member state.

credential.subtype: UN laissez-passer or Council of Europe laissez-passer

conditional_access.conditions: International organization staff holding official UN or Council of Europe laissez-passer are exempt from visa requirement.

credential.subtype: permanent or temporary residence permit issued by the Civil Registry and Migration Department

conditional_access.conditions: All persons who possess a permanent or temporary residence permit issued by the Civil Registry and Migration Department of Cyprus are exempt from the visa requirement.

credential.subtype: work permit or entry permit issued by the Civil Registry and Migration Department

conditional_access.conditions: All persons who are in possession of a work permit or entry permit issued by the Civil Registry and Migration Department of Cyprus are exempt from the visa requirement.

Holders of diplomatic passports of these specific countries are exempt from visa requirement. Ordinary passport holders of Azerbaijan are NOT visa-free and are also excluded from the Schengen/Bulgarian/Croatian/Romanian credential exemption.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports of these thirteen countries are exempt from visa requirement. Ordinary passport holders of Georgia, Moldova, Serbia, Ukraine have ordinary biometric passport visa-free access. Armenia, China, Iran, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Mongolia, Russia ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of diplomatic, service, and special passports of these seven countries are exempt from visa requirement. Ordinary UAE passport holders also have ordinary passport visa-free access; Egypt, Kuwait, Lebanon, Morocco, Oman, Syria ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports of these nineteen countries are exempt from visa requirement. UAE is listed in both this category and the diplomatic/service/special category above. Ordinary passport holders of Colombia, Mexico, Panama, Marshall Islands, Kiribati, Peru, Tonga, Palau, St. Lucia, Dominica, Grenada, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Trinidad and Tobago also have ordinary passport visa-free access. Cuba, India, Tuvalu, Vanuatu ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of diplomatic or official passports of Vietnam, South Korea, Pakistan, and Bahrain are exempt from visa requirement. South Korea ordinary passport holders also have ordinary passport visa-free access. Vietnam, Pakistan, Bahrain ordinary passport holders require a visa.

All flight crew members holding a Flight Crew Member's License or Crew Member Certificate issued under the Chicago Convention are exempt from visa requirement. Also covers flight crew and attendants on emergency or rescue flights, and civilian crew of ships.

cbi.notes: The Cyprus Investment Programme (Citizenship by Investment) was terminated effective November 1, 2020 by Council of Ministers decision dated October 13, 2020. In December 2025 (effective December 12, 2025), Cyprus formally abolished the underlying law, removing the Cabinet's discretion to grant nationality to investors. The European Commission formally closed the infringement case in March 2026. No CBI program is currently active.

programs.name: Cyprus Investor Immigration Permit (Regulation 6(2) – Permanent Residence by Investment)
programs.notes: 4th Revision criteria in force from May 2, 2023 (page last updated March 18, 2026). Four qualifying investment categories each requiring EUR 300,000 (plus VAT where applicable): (A) new residential property (first sale from developer), (B) non-residential or resale real estate, (C) share capital of a Cyprus-registered company employing at least 5 people, (D) units in a licensed Cyprus Investment Organisation of Collective Investments (AIF/AIFLNP/RAIF). Applicant must also demonstrate a secured annual income of at least EUR 50,000 from abroad (plus EUR 15,000 per dependent spouse, EUR 10,000 per minor child; income from Cypriot sources permitted for categories B, C, D). Application fee EUR 500 plus EUR 70 per person for ARC. Estimated examination period approximately 2 months from complete application. The permit is of unlimited validity; the residence card is issued for 10 years and renewed on expiry. Minor dependent children's cards valid until age 18. Adult children aged 18-25 who are tertiary students abroad may apply separately. Non-financially-dependent adult children can be included at a proportionally higher investment (EUR 300,000 multiplied by number of adult children). Annual monitoring of investment maintenance required; criminal record certificate every 3 years. Permit lapses if holder fails to enter Cyprus within 1 year of approval or is absent for 2 years.

programs.name: Cyprus Digital Nomad Visa Scheme
programs.eligibility: Third-country (non-EU/EEA) nationals who work remotely using telecommunications technology for employers or clients located abroad. Maximum of 500 permits total.
programs.processing_time: Within 3 months of arrival applicant must submit for temporary residence permit at Migration Department Nicosia
programs.notes: Minimum monthly net income of EUR 3,500 (after taxes/contributions); increased by 20% for a spouse/partner and 15% per child. Residence granted for 1 year, renewable for up to 2 further years. Maximum 500 permits. Family members may reside but cannot work or perform economic activity in Cyprus.

programs.name: Business Facilitation Unit (BFU) Fast-Track Highly Qualified Employees Scheme
programs.eligibility: Highly skilled third-country nationals employed by companies registered with the Business Facilitation Unit (Companies of Foreign Interests). No labour market test required. Valid until 31 December 2026.
programs.notes: Companies registered with the BFU (Ministry of Energy, Commerce and Industry) can freely employ any number of highly skilled third-country nationals without undergoing a labour market test. Applications processed by the Civil Registry and Migration Department within one month. Companies gain tax and other benefits for staff. Eligible until December 31, 2026.

programs.name: EU Blue Card Cyprus
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals (non-EU/EEA) with a third-level education qualification or equivalent professional experience, holding a valid work contract in Cyprus in eligible sectors (ICT, pharmaceuticals for research purposes, shipping excluding captains/crew). Minimum gross annual salary EUR 43,632.
programs.processing_time: Applications accepted from July 7, 2025; standard processing timeframe not specified on official page
programs.notes: Launched July 7, 2025. Minimum gross annual salary set at EUR 43,632. No maximum quota. Eligible sectors per Ministerial Decree: ICT, pharmaceuticals (for research purposes only), shipping (excluding ship captains and crew). Provides EU-wide intra-EU mobility rights (excluding Denmark and Ireland). Family reunification permits equal in duration to Blue Card. Enables accumulation of periods of residence across member states for EU long-term resident status. Application form MBCEU1 available on the Migration Department website.

programs.name: Fast-Track Naturalisation for Highly Skilled Employees
programs.eligibility: Highly skilled foreign employees of Companies of Foreign Interests (registered with BFU) with legal residence in Cyprus. Must meet amended Civil Registry Law criteria.
programs.processing_time: Maximum 8 months (via fast-track channel with EUR 5,000 fee)
programs.notes: Amendments gazetted December 19, 2023, reduced the previous 7-year standard residency requirement to 3-4 years for highly skilled workers of designated companies. Fast-track naturalisation capped at 8 months processing with a EUR 5,000 fee. Access via the Civil Registry and Migration Department.

validation_notes: CORRECTED region from 'Asia' to 'Europe' - Cyprus is an EU member state; its standard geopolitical classification is Europe.

ADDED Kosovo (XKX) to visa_free list - appears on official MFA page under 'Entities and territorial authorities not recognised as States by at least one member state of the EU' with a linked PDF on visa liberalisation; biometric passport required. Not in prior record.
ADDED Taiwan to visa_free list - appears on the official no-visa page under 'Entities and territorial authorities not recognised as States by at least one EU member state'. Not in prior record.

ADDED two new conditional_access entries: (a) Cyprus residence/temporary permit holders and (b) Cyprus work permit/entry permit holders - both explicitly listed on the official MFA no-visa page but absent from prior record.

ADDED flight crew conditional_access entry - all flight crew with ICAO Annex 1 licence or crew member certificate, plus emergency flight crew and civilian ship crew, explicitly exempt on official page.

UPDATED UK visa_free entry notes to explicitly mention British Nationals (Overseas), British Overseas Territories Citizens, British Overseas Citizens, British Protected Persons, and British Subjects - all listed on the official no-visa page.

CORRECTED diplomatic/official/service passport list in conditional_access: prior record listed 18 countries in that category; official page also lists 'United Arab Emirates' in that group (in addition to UAE already appearing in the diplomatic/service/special category), making 19 countries. Updated notes and count accordingly.
UPDATED RBI notes to confirm estimated 2-month examination period (from official page) and clarified annual income sourcing rules by investment category.

UPDATED EU Blue Card notes: official press release confirms shipping sector excludes 'ship captains and crew'; corrected notes which previously said 'maritime' without this exclusion. Removed unverified '90 days / 30 days' processing times not stated on the official press release.
ADDED 'mip-md.gov.cy' to official_domains - the investor immigration permit URL uses this subdomain.

ADDED visa_types field with 12 entries covering: tourist (Type C), transit (Type A), business (Type C), work (standard employment), EU Blue Card, BFU Fast-Track, student, digital nomad, investment (Regulation 6(2)), retirement (Category F), family reunification, and diplomatic waiver. Sourced from official Cyprus MFA and Migration Department pages previously accessed.

2026-06-27 REVALIDATION: gov.cy domain still returns HTTP 403 to automated fetches; all mfa.gov.cy subdomains redirect to gov.cy (also 403). No official page reachable to independently re-confirm the full nationality lists. No change made to visa_free, conditional_access, or visa_required lists - safety rule applied: preservation beats guessing. WebSearch and third-party sources (gk-lawfirm.com, etias.com, visahq.com) confirm Cyprus is NOT yet in Schengen as of June 2026 - EU Council unanimous vote anticipated by December 2026; Cyprus's independent national visa policy (including its own visa-free list) therefore remains in full force. No source found indicating any nationality added to or removed from the visa-free list since the last validation. CBI remains terminated (confirmed via multiple legal/news sources). No e-visa or ETA system has been introduced.

2026-06-29 REVALIDATION: gov.cy domain continues to return HTTP 403 to all automated WebFetch calls; mfa.gov.cy subdomains redirect to gov.cy (also 403). The Cyprus MFA Helsinki Embassy page and Beirut Embassy page also return 403. UK FCO travel advice (gov.uk) and gk-lawfirm.com (citing European Commission May 2026 Schengen report) confirm Cyprus has NOT yet joined the Schengen Area as of June 2026 - accession requires unanimous EU Council vote which has not yet occurred. Cyprus's independent national visa policy therefore remains fully in force. No source indicates any nationality added to or removed from the visa-free list. No e-visa or ETA system has been introduced. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is now confirmed for Q4 2026 launch, with mandatory enforcement expected April 2027; Cyprus is a participating member state. When ETIAS launches, visa-exempt third-country nationals visiting Cyprus will need to obtain an ETIAS authorization (EUR 20 fee, valid 3 years or passport expiry). This does NOT change the visa_free classification - ETIAS is an authorization, not a visa, and visa-free nationals remain visa-free; an eta entry should be added once ETIAS formally goes live and applies to Cyprus. No changes made to core visa_policy, conditional_access, cbi, rbi, or fast_track data - preservation rule applied.
gaps: The Kosovo visa-liberalisation PDF (https://www.gov.cy/media/sites/19/2024/06/Visa-liberalization-for-Kosovo-passport-holders.pdf) was not directly fetched; its existence is confirmed by the hyperlink on the no-visa page.
No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists for Cyprus per available official sources - confirmed absence recorded.
Max stay days for EU/EEA/Swiss nationals is not specified on the official page (freedom of movement applies - effectively unlimited).

No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder conditional exemption was found on any official Cyprus government source. India appears ONLY under the diplomatic/official/service passport waiver; ordinary Indian passport holders require a visa.
Path to citizenship for Investor Immigration Permit holders: no official direct path from Regulation 6(2) permit to citizenship confirmed in currently accessible official sources.
Specific bilateral agreement dates for diplomatic/service/special passport waivers were not directly confirmed from a scraped official treaty registry.
BFU scheme validity (until 31 December 2026) not re-verified from the BFU page in this session; carried forward from prior record.
Digital Nomad Visa Scheme 500-permit cap status (whether cap has been reached) was not checked in this validation session.
Fast-track naturalisation 3-4 year reduced residency requirement not re-verified directly on the Civil Registry page in this session.

gov.cy domain returns HTTP 403 to WebFetch - all official page fetches blocked in both prior and 2026-06-27 sessions; visa_policy data compiled from existing file data previously sourced from official pages.
Category F retirement permit fee schedule and exact income thresholds not re-verified in this session (gov.cy blocked).
Family reunification waiting period of 1 year of sponsor residency not re-verified in this session.

FUTURE WATCH (2026-06-27): Cyprus Schengen accession expected to be decided by EU Council by December 2026. If approved, Cyprus's independent national visa-free list will be superseded by Schengen Annex II - the visa_free list in this file would need a full re-scrape against the new Schengen visa-free framework. Monitor for Council decision.

FUTURE WATCH (2026-06-29): ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) confirmed for Q4 2026 launch with mandatory enforcement from April 2027. Cyprus is a participating member state. When ETIAS is mandatory, an 'eta' entry for ETIAS-required nationalities (60+ visa-exempt non-EU countries) should be added to visa_policy.eta. Currently not added because ETIAS is not yet operational. Note: ETIAS is an authorization system, not a visa - visa-free classification remains correct, but the 'eta' array will need population once ETIAS goes live.
FUTURE WATCH (2026-06-29): BFU Fast-Track scheme expires December 31, 2026 - verify whether it has been extended beyond that date in the next validation cycle.

gov.cy domain continues to return HTTP 403 to WebFetch in the 2026-06-29 session - three consecutive sessions (original data gathering, 2026-06-27 revalidation, 2026-06-29 revalidation) have all been blocked. No visa_policy data can be independently re-fetched from official sources.
